subject: Resource underutilization at MAX_CONCURRENT=8
body: Five concurrent jobs at the current cap consume ~26% of 32 logical threads and ~28% of 128 GB RAM (per-job footprint: ~3-4 GB, ~1-2 effective cores — well below RAYON_NUM_THREADS=4). The PROTOCOL.md fan-out table (Mac-derived, suggesting ~4 concurrent) is conservative for this Ryzen 7950X3D. South estimates 12-16 concurrent jobs would still leave headroom. Want me to bump MAX_CONCURRENT? Currently moot since the queue is dry.

subject: RE: bump MAX_CONCURRENT
body: Bumping to 12 now. Will report empirical CPU/RAM once 12 are running. Acked findings re doe-003 vs doe-001 amplitude saturation; will keep an eye on doe-006/010/011 for the LF capacitor->Randles transition.

subject: Empirical 10-sim resource data
body: 10 concurrent sims (5 LF + 4 mid + 1 ultra-LF) at MAX_CONCURRENT=12: CPU 54% of 32 threads (~17 effective), 1.9 GB RAM/sim (19 GB total), 37 GB system RAM used. Scaling 5->10 sims was ~linear in both CPU and RAM. Extrapolating: 12 sims ~65% CPU, ~23 GB sim-RAM; 16 sims ~85% CPU which is starting to crowd. cap=12 is a sensible ceiling for this hardware; bumping past ~16 risks CPU saturation.
